reviews.review_description:
For starters I’ve played all of the vr basketball games and this is the only game that is all about basketball. Hoopzter is the only game that has all of the games like vs, horse, 3 point challenge, knockout, king of the court and more. I don’t know how anyone could rate this as a bad game. It literally has everything . Here are some of my top reasons why I love the game and no other basketball games even compares. 
1. I can spin the ball and multiple balls
2. I can shatter the glass on my dunks
3. The graphics and details are the best
4. The Spatial Audio works perfectly
5. The courts are the best and are super animated
6. The cosmetics are very cheap so I can buy a lot of stuff 
7. My game gets better for real life because the physics are 100% realistic
8. The dribbling is easy
9. The avatars are meta and free 
10. The dunk animations are amazing and the power dunk and super jump are awesome
11. I can sit or stand 
12. They constantly update the game so every week it just gets better and better better. The list goes on and on about this game. I feel like they really pay attention to the culture. Hoopzter 4 life! Let’s go!!!

I really like this game and it is more on the chill and challenging side rather than tough and incomplete. It is based on skill rather than real life abilities. Get this game first and try it. You will not want to try out any other basketball games!!!!!!!😊😊😊😊😁😁😁😁😁

reviews.review_title: Nice game with some flaws here and there
reviews.review_description: Firstly, great game but greatness comes with flaws too the main thing/ concept that kinda takes away from the gameplay is this function that was added now I don’t know if this was a actual concept for the game or just a glitch but it’s remained in the game for I’d say about little over a month now and it gets pretty annoying with more and more people spamming it. Also I’d like to report the whole thing with super shoes and stuff I personally think that super shoes kinda take away from the whole realistic basketball essence being captured here. With these it’s fun but without you make it pretty unenjoyable with them forcing the player to pay for it which comes in the “pay-to-win” concept only being able to actually even reach or look over the without the shoes you can’t run fast or really even dunk witch really separates the game from others like Gym Class and Big Ballers Vr the 2 most popular vr games of all time where you can at least dunk freely without payment like actually sprinting too honestly stuff like this really takes away realistic basketball and fun it would be nice to rethink these thank you ( all love though hoopzterz vr!!!!❤️🏀!!!!)

Hoopzster Basketball is an exhilarating game
 that truly captures the essence of basketball. I love the fast-paced gameplay and the realistic graphics that make every match feel immersive. The controls are intuitive, allowing for smooth dribbling and shooting, which enhances the overall experience. The variety of game modes keeps things fresh, and I appreciate the opportunity to play with friends or compete against others online. Overall, Hoopzster Basketball is a fantastic choice for any basketball enthusiast looking for fun and competitive gameplay!

The game is good, in my honest opinion the physics of the dunking is impeccable and impressive since it has not only one dunking feature but two, due to the power dunk effect. Now as far as the dribbling and picking up is sufficient since it can be tweaked and improved but as far as playing it’s fun. Now some major changes i recommend is definitely the ability to not have so much movement but a restriction in place for the lack of traveling and also i would like to see a more stricter out of bounds since standing with the ball in out of bounds is allowed. However, besides that the game is an overall great game.

So, this game is pretty fun, but all the glitches/bugs just suck, Ive been playing on and off for a while now, and all they add is stupid things that have appeal to children, like dunk "packages" that are free but, balls, and more things that you have to buy such as jump boost and speed (which I have requested they make a way to disable it and/or make rooms that has it disabled) but anyways I'll list MY opinion of the games overall functions 
Dribbling: 6/10 (kinda wonky and too much assist) 
Rebounding: 2/10 (you can't bend down, you have to hold your hand in a above the ball and hold grip to rebound, and because of that people can just hit where your going to pick up the ball so it knocks it out. To be honest I could keep going on and on about the rebounding because it's so bad
Shooting: 8/10 pretty good, no aim assist, but the spin of the ball is a little weird
Dunking: 3/10 it's kinda dumb, your hand can be turned upside down and you can slam the ball into the side of the rim and it will still go in😭😭(not to mention there's no up and down or any rules to you can just jump around and play keep away, WHICH I ALSO requested to add maybe a little jump cool down or once they jump give them a smaller jump till they wait the cool down) 
Overall still a decent game the owner/devs just need to focus on more balancing.

developer_response.body:
Hey, really appreciate you taking the time to write such a detailed review — this kind of feedback genuinely helps us improve the game.

Just to clarify a few things and share what we’re working on:

Speed boost is optional — it only activates when using the front trigger, so you can play without it.

Jump height is controlled by how long you press the A button, so tapping it keeps your jump low and more controlled.

We’re pushing weekly updates, with a big focus right now on bug fixes, balancing, and improving overall feel.

We’re also adding systems like a shot clock and exploring a stamina system, which will help eliminate the “keep away” issue.

Regarding rebounding / picking up the ball — we understand exactly what you’re describing with having to position your hand above the ball.
👉 This is something we’re actively working on improving to make it feel more natural and responsive.

Dribbling assist is intentionally not 100%, so players still have control — but we’re continuing to tune that balance.

The ball spin is designed to follow real shooting mechanics and rotates correctly based on how a basketball is actually released.

For dunking, we’ve added a lot of depth (tricks, alley-oops, power dunks with glass shattering, rim effects, etc.), but we’re always looking to improve the overall feel and experience.

Totally hear you on the need for better balance overall — that’s something we’re continuously dialing in.

Appreciate the honest feedback — we’re building and improving Hoopzter every single week.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback — glad you like the game!

Just to clarify, boosts are optional features that some players choose to use, but they’re not required to play or compete. We also recently added the ability to toggle Super Jump on or off, so players can control how they want to play.

We also have plenty of game modes focused purely on skill, like HORSE, PIG, Around the World, 3-Point Challenge, Best of 10, and Knockout.

We’re always working on balancing everything to keep the experience fun and fair for everyone. Appreciate you playing and sharing your thoughts 🙏

developer_response.body:
Thanks for playing and leaving feedback.

The grip and ball control system is designed to simulate real hand mechanics in VR, so there can be a small learning curve at first. Once players get comfortable with the control timing, the handling becomes very smooth and responsive.

If you’re still having issues, feel free to reach out — we’re always happy to help players get dialed in.

The shooting in the game is so unrealistic, you can't even flick your wrist, it just feels like your throwing the ball instead of shooting the ball.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback. Hoopzter uses a physics-based shooting system designed to follow your real arm motion rather than using button timing or artificial aim assist. Because of this, the ball reacts directly to how you move and release the ball.

The system works very similarly to real basketball. If you shoot slightly to the left, the ball will go left. If you shoot straight, the ball goes straight. If you push the ball too hard, it will travel too far. In other words, the outcome is based entirely on the player’s motion and release rather than the game automatically correcting the shot.

Unlike many basketball games, Hoopzter intentionally does not use aim assist, so shots rely completely on the player’s technique. It can feel different at first if you're used to arcade-style basketball games, but many players end up enjoying the realism once they get comfortable with the shooting motion. We appreciate the feedback and are always working to improve the experience.

reviews.review_description: there is way to much backspin and dosent work for just shooting but the dunking is cool, also the ball just leaves your hand even gripping the ball and everything overall fix the shooting

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback — we really appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts.

Our shooting system is built around realistic basketball physics and proper technique. In real basketball, correct shooting form includes backspin, which is why the ball behaves that way in the game. The physics are designed to respond naturally to how you release the ball — if you shoot straight, it goes straight; if you shoot hard, it travels farther; if you shoot soft, it travels shorter.

Hoopzter is designed to reward good mechanics and practice. The more you play and refine your release, the more consistent and accurate your shots will feel.

We’re always working to improve the experience, so your feedback helps. Keep practicing and let us know how it feels as you get more reps in.

developer_response.body: Sorry you had that experience. Hoopzter includes both Mute and Block features that allow players to instantly silence or hide disruptive users. These options are available above player avatars and in the settings menu. While we can't control every player's behavior, we do provide tools to help create the experience you want. Thanks for the feedback.

developer_response.body:
Hey! 😄 Appreciate you trying Hoopzter — and sorry about the light bulb!

Quick tip: Hoopzter is actually designed so you can play sitting down. All movement like running, turning, and jumping is handled with the joysticks, so you don’t need a big space or high ceiling to enjoy the game.

That said, if you do go for full dunk mode standing up… things can get a little intense 😅

Thanks for the feedback — and we’re always working to improve the experience!

developer_response.body:
Hey, sorry to hear you had a bad experience.

If you’re open to it, we’d really like to understand what specifically made it feel that way so we can continue to improve. Hoopzter is designed to be a realistic, physics-based basketball experience, so things like shooting and movement are meant to feel authentic rather than arcade-style.

Also, just a heads up — the game can be played sitting down, and all movement (running, jumping, turning) is controlled with the joysticks, which helps with comfort and control.

We’re always open to feedback and continuously working to make the game better. Appreciate you giving it a try 🙏
